name: ALiBi
oneliner: Adds a fixed linear penalty to attention scores based on query-key distance — no position embeddings at all, with good length extrapolation.
explanation: Instead of position embeddings, ALiBi subtracts m_h·|i-j| from attention logits, where m_h is a head-specific slope. Nearby tokens get small penalty, distant tokens get large penalty. Slopes are a geometric sequence: m_h = 2^(-8/H · h), not learned. Primary motivation: length extrapolation — models trained at 1024 tokens work at 2048+ with minimal degradation, unlike learned/sinusoidal embeddings. Used by BLOOM, Falcon, MPT. Less common now due to RoPE's dominance + scaling extensions. Drawback: fixed monotonic decay is rigid.
fundamentals: Modified attention: a_{i,j} = $q_i^T$·$k_j$ - m_h·|i-j|. For H=8 heads: slopes = {1/2, 1/4, 1/8, ..., 1/256}. Steep slopes = strong locality, gentle slopes = far attention. Causal: bias = -m_h·(i-j) for j≤i. The bias matrix is lower-triangular. $O(1)$ additional params (fixed slopes). Extrapolation works because linear function is defined for any distance — no OOD issue. Cannot learn to attend strongly to a specific far position through positional info alone (content can override).
sources: Press et al., 'Train Short, Test Long,' ICLR 2022, arXiv:2108.12409

foundational_papers.title: Train Short, Test Long: Attention with Linear Biases Enables Input Length Generalization
